title: marina spiciformis
intro: Marina spiciformis, also known as the sea lavender or marsh sea lavender, is a low-growing, spreading, perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region and coastal areas of Europe, North Africa, and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant qualities. The health benefits of marina spiciformis include its ability to soothe respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of marina spiciformis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
